description: Fix the usage refresh error handling fallback in connectionUsageRefresh.ts to treat ALL 5xx errors (500-599) as transient (same as 502/504), remove dead code isConfirmedAuthBlockedError check in the fallback, and fix 429 handling to keep eligible connections clean.
steps: Read the full error handling section in src/lib/connectionUsageRefresh.ts lines 940-1030 to understand the current fallback logic after authBlockedPatch and quotaSignal checks.

In the final else branch of the `authBlockedPatch || { ... }` ternary (the fallback at ~line 960-1018), replace the three-way ternary (429 / 502|504 / other) with the following logic:

1. For status 429: If connection was eligible (routingStatus is 'eligible' or absent/unknown), write ONLY { routingStatus: 'eligible', lastCheckedAt, ...getOperationalUsageSnapshot(...) }. If NOT eligible, write { routingStatus: preservedStatus, lastCheckedAt, ...getOperationalUsageSnapshot(...) } (no nextRetryAt, no resetAt - the usage scheduler handles its own retry timing).

2. For ALL status 500-599 (not just 502/504): Use the same preserve-eligible pattern that currently exists for 502/504. If eligible, write clean { routingStatus: 'eligible', lastCheckedAt, ...getOperationalUsageSnapshot(...) }. If not eligible, write degraded with transient_upstream_error reason.

3. For other errors (non-5xx, non-429, non-auth): Keep the blocked fallback but REMOVE the dead isConfirmedAuthBlockedError check (it's unreachable because getConnectionAuthBlockedPatch already handled auth errors above). Use reasonCode: 'usage_request_failed' directly.
Replace the inline ternary with the refactored code. The syncUsageStatus call should use: `await syncUsageStatus(connection, authBlockedPatch || fallbackPatch);`
Run bun run typecheck to verify no type errors.

Run existing unit tests: bun vitest run --reporter=verbose --root tests tests/unit/status-handling-transient-auth.test.ts tests/unit/connection-status.test.ts tests/unit/connection-effective-status.test.ts tests/unit/codex-preemptive-cooldown.test.ts tests/unit/codex-provider-limits-utils.test.ts
acceptance_criteria: bun run typecheck passes with zero errors
All existing unit tests pass (the pre-existing model-combo-mapping-routes failure is unrelated and acceptable)
The fallback in connectionUsageRefresh.ts treats ALL 5xx (500-599) the same as 502/504 - keeping eligible connections clean
The dead isConfirmedAuthBlockedError check in the fallback is removed
429 in usage refresh does NOT write nextRetryAt or resetAt on eligible connections
The blocked fallback only fires for genuinely unknown errors (non-5xx, non-429, non-auth)
findings: Removed unused isConfirmedAuthBlockedError import since it was only used in the dead fallback code. All 68 tests pass cleanly. The refactored code uses if/else-if/else structure instead of the nested ternary, making it easier to maintain.
